manual_intro: Geelong's snow guide is strongly Victorian, with the central and north-eastern alpine resorts doing most of the work. Use this as a mix of family snow-play, resort weekends and more experienced high-country options.
place_description: Major full-service Victorian alpine resort with village stays, lifts, lessons and snow play. Best time: June to late September, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Winter entry, parking and chain rules apply during declared season.

manual_intro: Geelong's snow guide is strongly Victorian, with the central and north-eastern alpine resorts doing most of the work. Use this as a mix of family snow-play, resort weekends and more experienced high-country options.
place_description: Quieter Buller-adjacent snow-play and cross-country touring area with a nature-first feel. Best time: June to late September, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Carry chains in season and check whether the snow-play areas are operating.

place_description: Melbourne-side alpine resort built around snow play, cross-country skiing and first-timer winter days. Best time: June to late September, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Check the daily snow, road and resort operations update before driving.

manual_intro: Geelong's snow guide is strongly Victorian, with the central and north-eastern alpine resorts doing most of the work. Use this as a mix of family snow-play, resort weekends and more experienced high-country options.
place_description: A close natural-snow summit near Warburton for simple snow play after cold winter systems. Best time: after winter cold snaps or fresh snow reports; snow is occasional and not guaranteed. Expect crowds and changing road conditions on fresh-snow weekends.

place_description: Large Victorian alpine resort known for reliable snow cover, village stays, downhill terrain and cross-country trails. Best time: June to late September, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Check current snow reports, road status, parking and chain requirements.

place_description: Relaxed alpine village with snow play, tobogganing and easy pairing with the Hotham corridor. Best time: June to late September, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Best used as a village base or Hotham add-on rather than a large downhill resort.

place_description: High-altitude Victorian resort with strong snow culture, ski terrain and nearby snow-play options. Best time: June to late September, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Weather, alpine roads and chain rules are essential planning checks.

manual_intro: Geelong's snow guide is strongly Victorian, with the central and north-eastern alpine resorts doing most of the work. Use this as a mix of family snow-play, resort weekends and more experienced high-country options.
place_description: Expansive Victorian high-country terrain suited to cross-country skiing, snowshoeing and experienced winter touring. Best time: June to late September, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Treat as weather-exposed backcountry and plan conservatively.
